interfaces.name: DecisionStatus
interfaces.signature: enum DecisionStatus: String, Codable, Sendable { case active, resolved }
interfaces.notes: Active counts toward free-tier cap; resolved frees a slot immediately.

interfaces.name: OutcomeClassification
interfaces.signature: enum OutcomeClassification: String, Codable, Sendable { case held, missed, mixed }
interfaces.notes: Resolving outcomes only; 'not yet' is a separate non-resolving reschedule action, never a case here.

interfaces.name: DecisionSurface
interfaces.signature: enum DecisionSurface: String, Codable, Sendable { case app, widget, appIntent }
interfaces.notes: Tagged on every DecisionMutationService write call, enabling one parametrized cap-enforcement test across all three surfaces.

interfaces.name: SubscriptionAccess
interfaces.signature: enum SubscriptionAccess: String, Codable, Sendable { case free, paid }
interfaces.notes: Normalized entitlement state cached for extensions.

interfaces.name: RevisitSchedule
interfaces.signature: struct RevisitSchedule: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { var dayComponents: DateComponents; var reminderHour: Int; var reminderMinute: Int; var timeZoneIdentifier: String }
interfaces.notes: Calendar-day-plus-local-time intent; never store an absolute fire Date as product truth.

interfaces.name: DecisionOption
interfaces.signature: struct DecisionOption: Codable, Sendable, Equatable, Identifiable { var id: UUID; var text: String }
interfaces.notes: Optional captured alternative, editable after save.

interfaces.name: DecisionRecord
interfaces.signature: struct DecisionRecord: Codable, Sendable, Identifiable, Equatable { let id: UUID; var entryNumber: Int; let createdAt: Date; var updatedAt: Date; var decisionText: String; var prediction: String; var context: String?; var options: [DecisionOption]; var confidence: Int?; var whatWouldChangeMyMind: String?; var schedule: RevisitSchedule; var status: DecisionStatus; var outcomeID: UUID? }
interfaces.notes: entryNumber assigned atomically inside DecisionMutationService.createDecision via repository.nextEntryNumber(), never requested separately by UI, never client-generated.

interfaces.name: OutcomeRecord
interfaces.signature: struct OutcomeRecord: Codable, Sendable, Identifiable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let decisionID: UUID; var classification: OutcomeClassification; var narrative: String; let resolvedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Created only via DecisionPolicy.resolve, invoked from DecisionMutationService, never constructed directly by UI.

interfaces.name: DecisionDraft
interfaces.signature: struct DecisionDraft: Sendable, Equatable { var decisionText: String = ""; var prediction: String = ""; var schedule: RevisitSchedule?; var context: String = ""; var options: [DecisionOption] = []; var confidence: Int? = nil; var whatWouldChangeMyMind: String = ""; var isValid: Bool { get } }
interfaces.notes: Local working state for Capture and the App Intent; never persisted until DecisionMutationService.createDecision succeeds, so cancel/force-quit leaves no phantom record.

interfaces.name: OutcomeDraft
interfaces.signature: struct OutcomeDraft: Sendable, Equatable { var narrative: String; var classification: OutcomeClassification }
interfaces.notes: Narrative stays local until resolve() succeeds; never incrementally mutates the stored decision. 'Not yet' bypasses this entirely and calls rescheduleDecision instead.

interfaces.name: DecisionStats
interfaces.signature: struct DecisionStats: Sendable, Equatable { var heldCount: Int; var missedCount: Int; var mixedCount: Int; var totalResolved: Int { get }; var rawFractionText: String { get }; var headlineAccuracy: Double? }
interfaces.notes: Computed fresh from resolved records every time, never a stored counter. headlineAccuracy nil below the low-sample threshold.

interfaces.name: PendingRevisitSummary
interfaces.signature: struct PendingRevisitSummary: Sendable, Equatable { var overdueCount: Int; var dueSoonCount: Int; var upcomingCount: Int; var mostUrgentDecisionID: UUID?; var mostUrgentIsOverdue: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Widget-safe summary — counts/due-ness only, never decision content. mostUrgentDecisionID drives the single tap target on both widget sizes.

interfaces.name: DecisionPolicyError
interfaces.signature: enum DecisionPolicyError: Error, Sendable { case requiredFieldsMissing; case activeDecisionCapReached(limit: Int); case decisionAlreadyResolved; case decisionNotFound; case notificationPermissionDenied; case entitlementUnavailable }
interfaces.notes: Typed domain errors for consistent UI handling across surfaces.

interfaces.name: NotificationPlanItem
interfaces.signature: struct NotificationPlanItem: Sendable, Equatable, Identifiable { var id: String; var decisionID: UUID; var fireDateComponents: DateComponents; var title: String; var body: String }
interfaces.notes: Plain-data request generated by policy; title/body stay privacy-safe (counts/due-ness only), snapshot-testable without touching UNUserNotificationCenter.

interfaces.name: NotificationPlan
interfaces.signature: struct NotificationPlan: Sendable, Equatable { var items: [NotificationPlanItem] }
interfaces.notes: Rolling window over the nearest 30 active decisions.

interfaces.name: DecisionPolicy
interfaces.signature: protocol DecisionPolicy: Sendable { func canCreateActiveDecision(activeCount: Int, access: SubscriptionAccess) -> Bool; func nextEntryNumber(existingMax: Int) -> Int; func makeDecisionRecord(from draft: DecisionDraft, entryNumber: Int, now: Date) -> DecisionRecord; func resolve(decision: DecisionRecord, draft: OutcomeDraft, now: Date) throws -> OutcomeRecord; func reschedule(decision: DecisionRecord, to schedule: RevisitSchedule, now: Date) throws -> DecisionRecord; func revisitFireDate(for schedule: RevisitSchedule, calendar: Calendar) -> Date; func notificationWindow(activeDecisions: [DecisionRecord], limit: Int, now: Date) -> NotificationPlan; func makePendingSummary(activeDecisions: [DecisionRecord], now: Date) -> PendingRevisitSummary; func computeStats(resolved: [DecisionRecord], outcomes: [OutcomeRecord]) -> DecisionStats; func lowSampleThreshold() -> Int }
interfaces.notes: Pure rule engine; Calendar/TimeZone always injected by the implementation, never .current, so DST/timezone behavior is deterministically testable.

interfaces.name: DecisionRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol DecisionRepository: Sendable { func fetchActive() async throws -> [DecisionRecord]; func fetchResolved() async throws -> [DecisionRecord]; func fetch(id: UUID) async throws -> DecisionRecord?; func fetchOutcome(decisionID: UUID) async throws -> OutcomeRecord?; func activeCount() async throws -> Int; func nextEntryNumber() async throws -> Int; func persist(_ decision: DecisionRecord) async throws; func persist(_ outcome: OutcomeRecord, for decisionID: UUID) async throws; func delete(id: UUID) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Pure storage boundary only — no policy, no notification/widget side effects, no pagination (deliberate non-goal given realistic record volumes). Backed by SwiftData or Core Data depending on the earlier spike outcome. DecisionMutationService is the only caller for writes.

interfaces.name: DecisionMutationService
interfaces.signature: protocol DecisionMutationService: Sendable { func createDecision(from draft: DecisionDraft, access: SubscriptionAccess, surface: DecisionSurface) async throws -> DecisionRecord; func resolveDecision(id: UUID, draft: OutcomeDraft, surface: DecisionSurface, now: Date) async throws -> DecisionRecord; func rescheduleDecision(id: UUID, schedule: RevisitSchedule, surface: DecisionSurface, now: Date) async throws -> DecisionRecord; func updateDecision(id: UUID, draft: DecisionDraft, surface: DecisionSurface) async throws -> DecisionRecord }
interfaces.notes: The single serialized write boundary. Every method composes DecisionPolicy validation + DecisionRepository.persist + NotificationScheduling.recomputeAndApply + WidgetRefreshing.reloadRevisitWidgets as one operation. ViewModels and CaptureDecisionIntent call only this for writes, never DecisionRepository directly.

interfaces.name: NotificationScheduling
interfaces.signature: protocol NotificationScheduling: Sendable { func authorizationStatus() async -> UNAuthorizationStatus; func requestAuthorization() async throws -> Bool; func apply(plan: NotificationPlan) async throws; func removeNotifications(for decisionID: UUID) async throws; func recomputeAndApply(activeDecisions: [DecisionRecord], policy: DecisionPolicy, now: Date) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Diffs NotificationPlan against UNUserNotificationCenter; recomputeAndApply is invoked from DecisionMutationService on every mutation and separately on app launch as the safety net.

interfaces.name: EntitlementSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct EntitlementSnapshot: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { var access: SubscriptionAccess; var refreshedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Written to App Group by the main app on every StoreKit transaction update and every foreground; extensions only ever read it.

interfaces.name: EntitlementStore
interfaces.signature: protocol EntitlementStore: Sendable { func currentSnapshot() async throws -> EntitlementSnapshot; func refreshFromStoreKit() async throws -> EntitlementSnapshot }
interfaces.notes: No custom grace-period/TTL logic — trust StoreKit 2's JWS-verifiable transaction state as-is.

interfaces.name: ExportService
interfaces.signature: protocol ExportService: Sendable { func exportDecisions(decisions: [DecisionRecord], outcomes: [OutcomeRecord]) async throws -> URL }
interfaces.notes: User-initiated, local-only.

interfaces.name: DeepLinkTarget
interfaces.signature: enum DeepLinkTarget: Sendable, Equatable { case revisit(decisionID: UUID); case resolvedDetail(decisionID: UUID); case missingDecision }
interfaces.notes: Resolved navigation outcome fed identically by onOpenURL, the notification delegate, and AppIntent completion. missingDecision covers a stale notification/widget link pointing at a deleted record.

interfaces.name: AppRouter
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class AppRouter { var selectedTab: AppTab; var ledgerPath: NavigationPath; var presentedSheet: SheetRoute?; private(set) var pendingDestination: DeepLinkTarget?; func routeToDecision(_ target: DeepLinkTarget) }
interfaces.notes: Locked as a concrete final class, not a protocol: @Observable cannot attach to a protocol, and TabView/NavigationStack bindings need a concrete Bindable type, so a protocol here would need an identical concrete implementation underneath while adding no real benefit for something with exactly one implementation. routeToDecision is a single atomic call (no separate enqueue/process steps a caller could invoke out of order) that internally sequences sheet-dismiss -> tab-switch -> path-push in that order, never simultaneous mutations in one state update. Only one pending destination is retained — last write wins if a second target arrives before the first resolves. Must behave identically on cold launch and warm foreground.

interfaces.name: PermissionBannerState
interfaces.signature: enum PermissionBannerState: Sendable, Equatable { case hidden, denied, revoked }
interfaces.notes: Driven by an active authorizationStatus recheck on every foreground, not just first-launch denial.

interfaces.name: LoadState
interfaces.signature: enum LoadState<Value>: Sendable where Value: Sendable { case idle, loading, loaded(Value), empty, failed(String) }
interfaces.notes: Shared UI-state pattern for every screen needing explicit empty/loading/error handling.

interfaces.name: SubscriptionTier
interfaces.signature: enum SubscriptionTier: String, Sendable { case free, paid }

interfaces.name: DecisionWriteError
interfaces.signature: enum DecisionWriteError: Error, Sendable { case requiredFieldsMissing; case activeDecisionCapReached(limit: Int); case decisionAlreadyResolved; case decisionNotFound; case notificationPermissionDenied; case entitlementUnavailable; case persistenceFailure }
interfaces.notes: Every DecisionMutationService caller (form, widget, Siri) must render activeDecisionCapReached identically.

interfaces.name: CalibrationStats
interfaces.signature: struct CalibrationStats: Sendable { let heldCount: Int; let missedCount: Int; let mixedCount: Int; var totalResolved: Int { get }; var rawFractionText: String { get }; var percentageText: String? { get } }
interfaces.notes: Computed fresh from fetchResolved() every time — never stored as a denormalized counter. percentageText is nil below DecisionPolicy.lowSampleThreshold().

interfaces.name: WidgetSummary
interfaces.signature: struct WidgetSummary: Sendable { let overdueCount: Int; let dueSoonCount: Int; let mostUrgentDecisionID: UUID?; let mostUrgentIsOverdue: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Widget's only data shape — counts/due-ness only, never decision text. mostUrgentDecisionID drives the single tap target on both small and medium sizes.

interfaces.name: AppTab
interfaces.signature: enum AppTab: Sendable { case ledger, calibration }
interfaces.notes: Only two tabs in v1.

interfaces.name: SheetRoute
interfaces.signature: enum SheetRoute: Identifiable, Sendable { case capture; case paywall(PaywallTrigger) }
interfaces.notes: An open Capture draft is intentionally discarded if a deep-link interrupts it — stated tradeoff, not an accident.

interfaces.name: RouteMode
interfaces.signature: enum RouteMode: Sendable { case revisit, detailReadOnly }
interfaces.notes: detailReadOnly is used when a stale notification points at an already-resolved decision.

interfaces.name: LedgerViewModel
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class LedgerViewModel { private(set) var state: LoadState<[DecisionRecord]>; func load() async; func refreshOnForeground() async; func resolvedFilterToggled(_ on: Bool) }
interfaces.notes: refreshOnForeground wired to root scenePhase observation, confirmed by both participants — a Siri- or widget-triggered write made while backgrounded must surface without a manual pull-to-refresh.

interfaces.name: CaptureViewModel
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class CaptureViewModel { var draft: DecisionDraft; private(set) var capBlocked: Bool; func refreshCapStatus() async; func save() async throws -> DecisionRecord }
interfaces.notes: capBlocked checked synchronously on sheet appear (before typing starts), then re-validated atomically inside DecisionMutationService.createDecision.

interfaces.name: RevisitViewModel
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class RevisitViewModel { let decision: DecisionRecord; var narrative: String; func resolve(as classification: OutcomeClassification) async throws; func notYet(newSchedule: RevisitSchedule) async throws }
interfaces.notes: resolve() and notYet() are separate methods, never a shared toggle, keeping the non-resolving action structurally distinct.

interfaces.name: assembledAccessibilityLabel
interfaces.signature: func assembledAccessibilityLabel(for decision: DecisionRecord, now: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> String
interfaces.notes: Single combined VoiceOver sentence for a ledger row, e.g. 'Entry 14. Should we hire the contractor. Due in 6 days.'

interfaces.name: CaptureDecisionIntent
interfaces.signature: struct CaptureDecisionIntent: AppIntent { @Parameter var decisionText: String; @Parameter var prediction: String; @Parameter var revisitInDays: Int; func perform() async throws -> some IntentResult }
interfaces.notes: Builds a DecisionDraft and calls DecisionMutationService.createDecision(from:access:surface: .appIntent) — same path, same DecisionWriteError.activeDecisionCapReached as the in-app form.

interfaces.name: PaywallTrigger
interfaces.signature: enum PaywallTrigger: Sendable, Equatable { case capCeiling; case lockedAnalytics }
interfaces.notes: Paywall is always contextual, never a standalone destination.

interfaces.name: DecisionCommandService
interfaces.signature: protocol DecisionCommandService: Sendable { func create(draft: DecisionDraft, access: SubscriptionAccess, surface: DecisionSurface, now: Date) async throws -> DecisionRecord; func update(_ decision: DecisionRecord, surface: DecisionSurface, now: Date) async throws -> DecisionRecord; func resolve(id: UUID, draft: OutcomeDraft, surface: DecisionSurface, now: Date) async throws -> DecisionRecord; func reschedule(id: UUID, schedule: RevisitSchedule, surface: DecisionSurface, now: Date) async throws -> DecisionRecord; func delete(id: UUID, surface: DecisionSurface) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Single write boundary. Owns transactional writes plus required side effects: notification recompute and widget timeline reload after every mutation.

interfaces.name: WidgetRefreshing
interfaces.signature: protocol WidgetRefreshing: Sendable { func reloadRevisitWidgets() }
interfaces.notes: Thin wrapper over WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines(ofKind:) so DecisionMutationService is unit-testable with a mock instead of touching WidgetKit directly.

interfaces.name: WidgetDeepLink
interfaces.signature: enum WidgetDeepLink { static func url(for decisionID: UUID) -> URL; static func decisionID(from url: URL) -> UUID? }
interfaces.notes: Single shared implementation, confirmed by both participants, so the widget's widgetURL(_:) and the app's onOpenURL parser structurally cannot diverge.

interfaces.name: CalibrationViewModel
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class CalibrationViewModel { private(set) var state: LoadState<DecisionStats>; func load() async; func refreshOnForeground() async }
interfaces.notes: Same cross-process staleness reasoning as LedgerViewModel.
